qa_pairs.question: Who can initially access a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) award on Alliance general-purpose clusters, and how can a Principal Investigator (PI) manage these access permissions?
qa_pairs.answer: By default, every role sponsored through a PI's Alliance CCDB registration and any co-PIs listed on their RAC application will have access to the RAC award. To manage specific users for an allocation, the PI must log in to https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/, navigate to 'My Account' -> 'Manage RAP Memberships', select the relevant Resource Allocation Project (RAP), and then add members individually using their CCRI or in bulk for other PIs' sponsored users and co-PIs.

qa_pairs.question: When submitting jobs to the scheduler on an Alliance general-purpose cluster, how should users specify the correct group name for jobs related to their RAC award versus other research?
qa_pairs.answer: When submitting jobs, users need to specify a group name as the value of the `--account` option. Jobs pertaining to the research described in the RAC application should be submitted with the group name corresponding to the RAC award (e.g., `--account=rrg-profname-ab`). Jobs pertaining to other research should be submitted with the default group name (e.g., `--account=def-profname`).

qa_pairs.question: How is a `/project` storage allocation structured on an Alliance general-purpose cluster for a RAC award, and what is its intended use?
qa_pairs.answer: A `/project` storage allocation on a general-purpose cluster for a RAC award is created as a directory, typically in the form `/project/<group-name>` (e.g., `/project/rrg-profname-ab`), and includes an associated quota defining the amount of data that can be stored. All sponsored users should store files pertaining to the research described in the RAC application within this directory. Users also have a default project space (e.g., `/project/def-profname`) and may choose to move relevant data from there to their RAC `/project` directory.
